examples.title: Check File Permission (Pseudo-code)
examples.description: Algorithm to check if a user has specific permission on a file using ACL
examples.code:
ALGORITHM CheckPermission
INPUT: user, file, requested_operation
OUTPUT: GRANTED or DENIED

BEGIN
  // Get user's group(s)
  user_groups = GetUserGroups(user)
  
  // Get ACL for the file
  file_acl = GetACL(file)
  
  // Check owner permissions
  IF user == file.owner THEN
    IF requested_operation IN file_acl.owner_permissions THEN
      RETURN GRANTED
    END IF
  END IF
  
  // Check group permissions
  FOR EACH group IN user_groups DO
    IF group == file.group THEN
      IF requested_operation IN file_acl.group_permissions THEN
        RETURN GRANTED
      END IF
    END IF
  END FOR
  
  // Check others permissions
  IF requested_operation IN file_acl.others_permissions THEN
    RETURN GRANTED
  END IF
  
  RETURN DENIED
END
examples.explanation: This pseudo-code demonstrates how file permission checking works in Unix-like systems. It checks owner first, then group, then others - the standard precedence in Unix.

examples.title: Unix Permission Calculator in Python
examples.description: Python program to calculate and display Unix file permissions
examples.code:
def permission_string(mode):
    """Convert numeric mode to rwx string"""
    perms = []
    # Owner permissions
    perms.append('r' if mode & 0o400 else '-')
    perms.append('w' if mode & 0o200 else '-')
    perms.append('x' if mode & 0o100 else '-')
    # Group permissions
    perms.append('r' if mode & 0o040 else '-')
    perms.append('w' if mode & 0o020 else '-')
    perms.append('x' if mode & 0o010 else '-')
    # Others permissions
    perms.append('r' if mode & 0o004 else '-')
    perms.append('w' if mode & 0o002 else '-')
    perms.append('x' if mode & 0o001 else '-')
    return ''.join(perms)

def numeric_to_octal(mode):
    """Convert rwx string to octal"""
    octal = 0
    if 'r' in mode[0:3]: octal += 4
    if 'w' in mode[0:3]: octal += 2
    if 'x' in mode[0:3]: octal += 1
    octal *= 100
    if 'r' in mode[3:6]: octal += 4
    if 'w' in mode[3:6]: octal += 2
    if 'x' in mode[3:6]: octal += 1
    octal *= 10
    if 'r' in mode[6:9]: octal += 4
    if 'w' in mode[6:9]: octal += 2
    if 'x' in mode[6:9]: octal += 1
    return octal

# Example usage
print(permission_string(0o755))  # Output: rwxr-xr-x
print(permission_string(0o644))  # Output: rw-r--r--
print(numeric_to_octal('rwxr-xr-x'))  # Output: 755
examples.explanation: This Python code demonstrates conversion between numeric (octal) and symbolic (rwx) Unix file permissions. The permission bits are: Read=4, Write=2, Execute=1, summed for each category.

examples.title: Capability List Implementation in C
examples.description: C program showing simple implementation of capability list for file access control
examples.explanation: This C code demonstrates a Capability List implementation where each user has a list of files they can access along with their permissions. This is the user-centric approach to file protection.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of capabilities

examples.title: ACL-based File Access Control
examples.description: Pseudo-code for implementing Access Control List with revocation support
examples.code:
ALGORITHM ACL_FileAccess
INPUT: user, file, operation
OUTPUT: Access granted or denied

DATA STRUCTURES:
  ACL_Entry = STRUCTURE
    user_id
    allowed_operations (set of {READ, WRITE, EXECUTE})
  END STRUCTURE

  File_ACL = STRUCTURE
    file_id
    entries (list of ACL_Entry)
  END STRUCTURE

BEGIN
  file_acl = GetFileACL(file.id)
  
  FOR EACH entry IN file_acl.entries DO
    IF entry.user_id == user.id THEN
      IF operation IN entry.allowed_operations THEN
        LogAccess(user, file, operation, GRANTED)
        RETURN GRANTED
      ELSE
        LogAccess(user, file, operation, DENIED)
        RETURN DENIED
      END IF
    END IF
  END FOR
  
  // No entry found - deny by default
  LogAccess(user, file, operation, DENIED)
  RETURN DENIED
END

// REVOCATION FUNCTION
PROCEDURE RevokeAccess(user_id, file_id)
BEGIN
  file_acl = GetFileACL(file_id)
  
  FOR EACH entry IN file_acl.entries DO
    IF entry.user_id == user_id THEN
      REMOVE entry FROM file_acl.entries
      LogRevocation(user_id, file_id)
      RETURN SUCCESS
    END IF
  END FOR
  
  RETURN NOT_FOUND
END
examples.explanation: This pseudo-code shows how ACL-based access control works, including the check process and revocation capability. It demonstrates both granting and revoking access rights.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of ACL entries
